tasks.description: Create project directories and configuration files for Module 1.

steps.description: Create directories: src/core, src/modules/prompt_gen, tests/core, tests/modules/prompt_gen
Create empty __init__.py files in all created directories
Create requirements.txt with google-genai, pydantic, python-dotenv, pytest, pytest-mock
Create .env.example with placeholders for GOOGLE_API_KEY and GEMINI_MODEL
Create .gitignore to exclude .env, __pycache__, and .pytest_cache

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Project structure exists. Requirements file lists all dependencies. Gitignore excludes sensitive files.

tasks.description: Develop a configuration manager that reads settings from Runtime, File, and Environment variables in priority order.

steps.description: Create src/core/config.py and tests/core/test_config.py
Implement ConfigManager class with methods to set/get API keys and models
Implement priority logic: Runtime args > config.json (simulated) > .env

tasks.acceptance-criteria: ConfigManager correctly resolves API key from highest priority source. Missing key raises appropriate error.

unit: Runtime injection overrides environment variable
Environment variable is used when runtime/file are missing
get_api_key returns correct value for all sources
get_model_name defaults to gemini-3.1-pro-preview if not set

tasks.description: Create strict Pydantic schemas for Prompt Input and Output to ensure JSON mode compatibility.

steps.description: Create src/modules/prompt_gen/schemas.py
Define PromptInput model (text_prompt, image_path optional)
Define PromptOutput model (positive_prompt, negative_prompt, style_parameters)

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Schemas validate correct input types and reject invalid data. PromptOutput schema can be exported to JSON schema format.

unit: PromptInput accepts valid text and optional image path
PromptOutput validation fails if positive_prompt is missing
PromptOutput serializes to expected JSON structure

tasks.description: Define an abstract base class to standardize the prompt generation interface across different providers.

steps.description: Create src/modules/prompt_gen/interface.py
Define abstract method generate(input: PromptInput) -> PromptOutput

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Abstract class prevents instantiation. Concrete subclasses must implement generate method.

unit: Cannot instantiate BasePromptGenerator directly
Subclass without generate method fails to instantiate

tasks.description: Develop the Gemini adapter to construct correct API requests using the Google GenAI SDK.

steps.description: Create src/modules/prompt_gen/gemini_adapter.py
Implement initialization logic using ConfigManager
Implement _construct_request method to prepare messages and config

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Adapter initializes with correct model. Request payload includes system instructions and JSON schema.

unit: Adapter initializes with API key from ConfigManager
System prompt is correctly formatted
Generation config includes response_mime_type='application/json'

tasks.description: Implement response handling logic to parse JSON output and handle API errors.

steps.description: Implement generate method in GeminiAdapter
Add error handling for API failures and malformed JSON
Convert raw JSON response to PromptOutput object

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Valid JSON response is converted to PromptOutput. Network errors or invalid JSON raise custom exceptions.

unit: Parses valid JSON string into PromptOutput object
Raises custom exception on API 500 error
Raises validation error if API returns non-conforming JSON
integration: Mocked client returns success and adapter returns valid object

tasks.description: Create a script to run a real end-to-end test against the Gemini API (requires valid API key).

steps.description: Create demo_module1.py in project root
Script should read real .env, instantiate adapter, and print result

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Script runs successfully with a valid API key and prints a structured PromptOutput.

e2e-manual: Run script with valid key -> verify JSON output matches pixel art requirements
Run script with invalid key -> verify error message

tasks.description: Add Pillow to requirements.txt for image saving and processing, and update environment variables.

steps.description: Add Pillow to requirements.txt
Update .env.example with GEMINI_IMAGE_MODEL placeholder

tasks.acceptance-criteria: requirements.txt includes Pillow and .env.example contains GEMINI_IMAGE_MODEL placeholder.

tasks.description: Define an abstract base class to standardize the image generation interface across different providers.

steps.description: Create src/modules/image_gen and tests/modules/image_gen directories with __init__.py
Create src/modules/image_gen/schemas.py to define ImageGenInput and ImageGenOutput
Create src/modules/image_gen/interface.py defining abstract method generate

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Directory structure is set up. Schemas enforce input and output types. Interface prevents direct instantiation.

unit: Schemas validation rejects incorrect types and validates expected schema
interface raises TypeError when instantiating BaseImageGenerator directly

tasks.task: Implement ConfigManager Support for Image Models
tasks.description: Update ConfigManager to handle different model keys for image generation.

steps.description: Add get_image_model() method to ConfigManager
Prioritize CLI runtime arg --image-model over GEMINI_IMAGE_MODEL in .env

tasks.acceptance-criteria: ConfigManager returns valid image_model_name from env or default string.

unit: get_image_model returns GEMINI_IMAGE_MODEL environment variable value
get_image_model returns default model name if env variable is absent
get_image_model respects explicit initialization override

tasks.task: Implement Gemini Image Generator Adapter API Logic
tasks.description: Build adapter utilizing google-genai SDK's models.generate_images to trigger image creation with proper payload.

steps.description: Create src/modules/image_gen/exceptions.py with ImageGenerationError
Create src/modules/image_gen/gemini_adapter.py extending BaseImageGenerator
Implement construction logic extracting prompts and configuring model generation params (e.g., aspect_ratio=1:1 and person_generation='allow_adult')

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Exception handler intercepts API errors. The generate function invokes the GenAI SDK with Fixed aspect_ratio strings.

unit: Adapter initialization fetches the api_key and correct image model name from config_manager
generate function delegates effectively to the client wrapper and catches API exceptions

tasks.task: Implement Image Save And Byte Encoding Logistics
tasks.description: Implement processing of GenerateImagesResponse to convert bytes to a saved PIL Image.

steps.description: Extract image bytes generated by Gemini adapter
Load bytes using Pillow Image module
Generate unique filesystem path and save as PNG

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Adapter successfully intercepts raw bytes, validates against PIL integrity, and commits output to filesystem.

unit: Mocked GenerateImagesResponse successfully routes byte data through PIL onto simulated directory
Throws custom ImageGenerationError on invalid/missing byte parsing
integration: Generated Image object commits to the filesystem structure with valid .png MIME format

tasks.description: Write an automation test file triggering generation on the actual Google backend.

steps.description: Create demo_module2.py in project root
Instantiate the module 2 adapter and invoke the generation interface with dummy outputs from Module 1

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Script runs end to end and downloads actual imagery successfully based on dummy prompts.

e2e-manual: Execute script against valid key -> verify 1024x1024 generated png is stored on disk properly
Inspect generated PNG manually to ensure dimensions and file data integrity

tasks.task: Define Pixelization Module Structure and Schemas
tasks.description: Create directories, interfaces, and Pydantic models for the Pixelization module.

steps.description: Create src/modules/pixelization and tests/modules/pixelization directories with __init__.py
Define PixelizationInput and PixelizationOutput Pydantic schemas in schemas.py
Define BasePixelization interface class in interface.py
Create exceptions.py for custom ComfyUI errors like connection and timeout errors

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Project structure exists for module 3. Schemas validate input image paths. Interface enforces the generate() method.

unit: PixelizationInput accepts valid local file paths and image byte streams
BasePixelization abstraction prevents direct instantiation

tasks.description: Extend ConfigManager to support ComfyUI URL and workflow template path configurations.

steps.description: Add COMFYUI_URL and COMFYUI_WORKFLOW_TEMPLATE to .env.example
Update ConfigManager to parse these values, prioritizing runtime args over environment variables

tasks.acceptance-criteria: ConfigManager returns valid ComfyUI endpoint URLs and template file paths.

unit: get_comfyui_url defaults to http://127.0.0.1:8000 if none provided
Runtime arguments override environment variables for comfyui_url

tasks.description: Develop a lower-level HTTP and WebSocket client to communicate with the ComfyUI API.

steps.description: Create comfyui_client.py to handle HTTP POST /prompt and POST /upload/image
Implement HTTP GET /history and GET /view to retrieve results
Implement WebSocket listener to block and track generation progress by prompt_id
Add configurable timeout logic to prevent infinite hangs

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Client can successfully upload images, queue workflows, track execution via WebSocket, and download resulting images with explicit timeout limits.

unit: Client HTTP methods successfully parse 200 OK responses with mock data
WebSocket listener correctly identifies completion message for specific prompt_id
Client throws specific ComfyUITimeoutException when generation exceeds timeout
integration: Client gracefully handles ConnectionRefusedError when local ComfyUI is offline

tasks.description: Build the ComfyUIAdapter class to translate inputs into a JSON workflow and orchestrate the API Client.

steps.description: Create comfyui_adapter.py implementing BasePixelization interface
Implement logic to load workflow_api.json template from disk
Inject dynamic variables (uploaded image filename, specific prompts, seed) into fixed Node IDs
Wrap generation execution with logging and map exceptions correctly

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Adapter successfully coordinates the payload generation, uses the client to execute the task, and returns a verified PixelizationOutput.

unit: Adapter generates correct workflow JSON with dynamic node values updated
generate method catches internal client errors and raises adapter-specific exceptions
integration: Adapter executes full lifecycle (upload -> prompt -> wait -> download) using a mocked client instance

tasks.description: Create a demonstrator script that processes a local image through the full ComfyUI pipeline.

steps.description: Create workflow_api_template.json with a valid ComfyUI Graph containing ControlNet and Checkpoint
Create demo_module3.py to load an image and invoke ComfyUIAdapter

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Script runs end-to-end against a running local ComfyUI instance and outputs a pixelated image to disk.

e2e-manual: Execute script while ComfyUI is running locally -> verify converted pixel art image is generated in output folder
Execute script while ComfyUI is stopped -> verify graceful connection error message is printed

tasks.task: Define Post-Processing Module Structure and Schemas
tasks.description: Create directories, Pydantic schemas, exceptions, and abstract interface for the post-processing module.

steps.description: Create src/modules/post_processing and tests/modules/post_processing directories with __init__.py
Define PostProcessingInput schema with image_path, asset_name, target_sizes, remove_background, color_count, palette_path, output_dir fields
Define PostProcessingOutput schema with output_paths, target_sizes, color_count, palette_name fields
Create exceptions.py with PostProcessingError, BackgroundRemovalError, ColorQuantizationError, DownscaleError
Create interface.py with BasePostProcessor abstract class defining process(input) -> output method

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Directory structure exists for module 4. PostProcessingInput validates target_sizes as non-empty list of positive integers. PostProcessingInput rejects palette_path and color_count both being None when quantization is implicitly expected. PostProcessingOutput serializes output_paths as list of strings. BasePostProcessor prevents direct instantiation and enforces process method.

unit: PostProcessingInput accepts valid image_path, asset_name, target_sizes=[32,64], remove_background=True, color_count=16
PostProcessingInput rejects empty target_sizes list
PostProcessingInput rejects target_sizes containing non-positive integers
PostProcessingInput accepts palette_path without color_count (palette mode)
PostProcessingInput accepts color_count without palette_path (K-Means mode)
PostProcessingOutput serializes output_paths as list of file path strings
BasePostProcessor raises TypeError on direct instantiation
Subclass without process method raises TypeError on instantiation

tasks.task: Update Dependencies and ConfigManager for Module 4
tasks.description: Add rembg and scikit-learn to requirements.txt and extend ConfigManager with post-processing default settings.

steps.description: Add rembg, scikit-learn, numpy to requirements.txt
Add POST_PROCESSING_OUTPUT_DIR, DEFAULT_COLOR_COUNT, DEFAULT_TARGET_SIZES, REMBG_MODEL to .env.example
Implement get_post_processing_output_dir method in ConfigManager with default './output/assets'
Implement get_default_color_count method in ConfigManager with default 16
Implement get_default_target_sizes method in ConfigManager returning list of ints with default [32, 64]

tasks.acceptance-criteria: requirements.txt includes rembg, scikit-learn, numpy. ConfigManager returns default output dir './output/assets' when no override exists. ConfigManager returns default color_count=16. ConfigManager returns default target_sizes=[32,64]. Runtime args override environment variables for all new settings.

unit: get_post_processing_output_dir returns './output/assets' when no config is set
get_post_processing_output_dir returns runtime override value when provided
get_default_color_count returns 16 when no config is set
get_default_color_count returns integer from environment variable string
get_default_target_sizes returns [32, 64] when no config is set
get_default_target_sizes parses comma-separated env string '32,64,128' into int list

tasks.description: Create a BackgroundRemover class wrapping the rembg library to remove image backgrounds and produce alpha-channel PNGs.

steps.description: Create src/modules/post_processing/background_remover.py
Implement remove method accepting PIL Image and returning PIL Image with RGBA alpha channel
Add configurable rembg model selection (default u2net)
Handle rembg errors and wrap them in BackgroundRemovalError

tasks.acceptance-criteria: BackgroundRemover.remove accepts an RGB PIL Image and returns an RGBA PIL Image. Output image has identical width and height to input. Alpha channel contains non-trivial values (not all 0 or all 255 for images with backgrounds). Rembg failures raise BackgroundRemovalError with descriptive message.

unit: remove returns RGBA mode image when given RGB input with mock rembg returning known alpha data
remove returns RGBA mode image when given RGBA input (already has alpha) with mock rembg
Output image dimensions match input image dimensions using mock rembg
remove raises BackgroundRemovalError when rembg throws an internal exception (mocked)
BackgroundRemover initializes with default model 'u2net' when no model specified
BackgroundRemover initializes with custom model name when explicitly provided

tasks.description: Create a PaletteLoader class to parse Lospec .hex palette files and provide built-in classic palettes like GB and NES.

steps.description: Create src/modules/post_processing/palette_loader.py
Implement load_from_hex_file method parsing .hex format (one hex color per line, e.g. 'FF5733')
Implement built-in presets dictionary with GB (4 colors), NES (54 colors), and Sweetie-16 (16 colors) palettes
Implement get_preset method returning palette by name
Validate that loaded palettes contain valid RGB tuples and have at least 2 colors

tasks.acceptance-criteria: PaletteLoader.load_from_hex_file correctly parses a .hex file into a list of (R, G, B) tuples. Built-in presets return correct color counts (GB=4, Sweetie-16=16). get_preset raises ValueError for unknown preset names. Palette with fewer than 2 colors raises ValueError. Hex strings with invalid characters raise ValueError.

unit:
load_from_hex_file parses '0f380f
306230
8bac0f
9bbc0f' into 4 correct RGB tuples
load_from_hex_file ignores empty lines and lines starting with '#' (comments)
load_from_hex_file raises ValueError for hex string with invalid characters like 'ZZZZZZ'
load_from_hex_file raises ValueError when file contains fewer than 2 valid colors
get_preset('gb') returns exactly 4 RGB tuples matching Game Boy green palette
get_preset('sweetie-16') returns exactly 16 RGB tuples
get_preset('nonexistent') raises ValueError with descriptive message
All built-in preset colors have R, G, B values in range 0-255

tasks.description: Create a ColorQuantizer class supporting K-Means auto-color selection and fixed-palette nearest-color mapping while preserving alpha channels.

steps.description: Create src/modules/post_processing/color_quantizer.py
Implement quantize_kmeans method using scikit-learn KMeans to reduce image to N colors
Implement quantize_palette method mapping each pixel to nearest color in a given palette using Euclidean distance
Implement quantize dispatch method that chooses kmeans or palette mode based on input
Preserve alpha channel during quantization by processing only RGB channels
Wrap scikit-learn errors in ColorQuantizationError

tasks.acceptance-criteria: quantize_kmeans reduces a multi-color image to exactly N unique colors. quantize_palette maps all pixels to the nearest palette color with zero off-palette colors in output. Alpha channel values are preserved identically before and after quantization. ColorQuantizationError is raised for invalid inputs (N<2, empty palette).

unit: quantize_kmeans with N=4 on a 10x10 image with 100 random colors produces output with at most 4 unique RGB values
quantize_kmeans with N=2 on a pure red/blue checkerboard returns exactly red and blue
quantize_kmeans raises ColorQuantizationError when N < 2
quantize_palette maps pure red pixel (255,0,0) to nearest palette color in [(200,0,0),(0,200,0),(0,0,200)]
quantize_palette with GB 4-color palette produces output containing only those 4 colors
quantize_palette output contains zero colors not present in the input palette
Alpha channel is preserved: RGBA image with alpha=128 region retains alpha=128 after quantize_kmeans
Alpha channel is preserved: RGBA image with alpha=0 region retains alpha=0 after quantize_palette
quantize dispatches to quantize_palette when palette is provided and to quantize_kmeans when only color_count is given
integration: ColorQuantizer with palette loaded from PaletteLoader.get_preset('gb') produces output with exactly 4 unique colors

tasks.description: Create a Downscaler class using PIL nearest-neighbor interpolation to resize images to exact target pixel dimensions.

steps.description: Create src/modules/post_processing/downscaler.py
Implement downscale method accepting PIL Image and target size, returning square resized image
Implement downscale_multi method accepting a list of target sizes and returning a list of resized images
Validate that only NEAREST resampling is used (never BILINEAR or LANCZOS)
Handle edge cases: target size larger than source, non-square source images

tasks.acceptance-criteria: downscale produces exact target dimensions (e.g. 32x32). Output uses NEAREST resampling only (no anti-aliased edges). downscale_multi returns correct number of images matching target sizes list. Non-square source images are handled by center-cropping or fitting to square. DownscaleError raised for invalid target sizes (0 or negative).

unit: downscale of 256x256 RGBA image to 32 produces exactly 32x32 image
downscale of 512x512 RGB image to 64 produces exactly 64x64 image
downscale preserves RGBA mode (does not drop alpha channel)
downscale using a known 4x4 checkerboard pattern scaled to 2x2 produces expected pixel values (no blending)
downscale_multi with [32, 64, 128] returns list of 3 images with matching dimensions
downscale raises DownscaleError when target size is 0
downscale raises DownscaleError when target size is negative
Non-square 256x128 image downscaled to 32 produces 32x32 output

tasks.description: Create the PostProcessingPipeline class that orchestrates background removal, color quantization, downscaling, and asset export based on input configuration.

steps.description: Create src/modules/post_processing/pipeline.py implementing BasePostProcessor
Implement process method loading input image and chaining steps based on PostProcessingInput flags
Implement step-skipping logic: skip background removal when remove_background=False, skip quantization when both color_count and palette_path are None
Implement asset naming convention: output files as {asset_name}_{size}x{size}.png
Create output directory if it does not exist
Populate PostProcessingOutput with all generated file paths and metadata

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Pipeline executes full chain (remove bg → quantize → downscale → save) when all options enabled. Pipeline skips background removal when remove_background=False. Pipeline skips color quantization when color_count is None and palette_path is None. Output files follow naming convention '{asset_name}_{size}x{size}.png'. Output directory is auto-created if missing. PostProcessingOutput contains correct list of output_paths matching target_sizes count.

unit: Pipeline with all steps enabled calls BackgroundRemover, ColorQuantizer, and Downscaler in correct order (mocked components)
Pipeline with remove_background=False skips BackgroundRemover (mocked, verify not called)
Pipeline with color_count=None and palette_path=None skips ColorQuantizer (mocked, verify not called)
Pipeline generates files named '{asset_name}_{size}x{size}.png' for each target_size (mocked filesystem)
Pipeline creates output_dir if it does not exist (mocked os.makedirs)
Pipeline returns PostProcessingOutput with output_paths matching number of target_sizes
integration: Pipeline with real BackgroundRemover (mocked rembg), real ColorQuantizer, and real Downscaler produces correct-dimension output files on temp filesystem
Pipeline processes a real 256x256 RGBA test image through full chain and saves to temp directory with correct filenames
Pipeline with palette_path pointing to a real .hex file quantizes using palette mode via PaletteLoader and ColorQuantizer

tasks.description: Create demo_module4.py to run the full post-processing pipeline on a pixelized image from Module 3 output.

steps.description: Create demo_module4.py in project root
Load a pixelized image from output/images directory as input
Configure PostProcessingInput with representative settings (remove_bg=True, 16 colors, sizes=[32,64])
Execute pipeline and print status at each processing stage
Verify all output files exist and print dimensions and file sizes

tasks.acceptance-criteria: Script runs end-to-end and produces correctly named PNG files in output/assets directory. Output images have exact target dimensions. Output images contain limited color palettes visible on inspection.

e2e-manual: Execute script with a pixelized source image -> verify 32x32 and 64x64 PNGs are generated in output/assets
Open output PNGs in image viewer -> verify transparent background (alpha channel) and limited color palette
Inspect output filenames match pattern '{asset_name}_32x32.png' and '{asset_name}_64x64.png'
Execute script with remove_background=False -> verify output retains original background
